title: Website Contact us Form - WordPress Plugin and Monday.com
excerpt: My team is migrating our website to WordPress and struggling to find a suitable plugin for our “Contact Us” form. We’re using the Avada theme but couldn’t find an easy way to connect the Avada form to monday.com through …

posts.author: addmeyer (Addie Meyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
My team is migrating our website to WordPress and struggling to find a suitable plugin for our “Contact Us” form. We’re using the Avada theme but couldn’t find an easy way to connect the Avada form to monday.com through a third-party integration.

As a small company with fewer than 200 leads per year, paying $100–$200 for plugins like WPForms or Gravity Forms feels excessive. Previously, we used monday Workforms, but since it doesn’t integrate with Google Tag Manager, tracking has been challenging.

For those with WordPress websites, what form plugin do you recommend that integrates easily with both monday.com and Google Tag Manager? We would the form to be able to connect to our CRM to create a new lead and if possible add an item to a board.

posts.author: inspiredearth (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Addie,
What solution did you figure out for this?
Jonathan

posts.author: dvdsmpsn (David @ David Simpson Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Why not simple add a monday WorkForm in an iframe on your contact us page?

Like this:

David Simpson Apps

Contact Us | David Simpson Apps

We'd love to hear from you! There are a bunch of ways to reach us, so pick the one that works best for you, and let's start the conversation!

posts.author: beverlyRoadGoose (Tobi)
posts.cookedHtml: You could also try using the Avada Post request Form option along with HookPanda to generate the link that receives the form data, and can create items from the received items.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml: Out of curiosity, is there a reason you’re not using a native monday WorkForm for this use case? Is it mainly due to tracking (GTM), website embedding, or something else?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @addmeyer You’re not doing anything wrong. Avada forms don’t connect cleanly to monday without custom work.

For a low lead volume, the simplest and cheapest option is Contact Form 7. It’s free, works well with Google Tag Manager, and can connect to monday via Make or Zapier using a webhook to create a new lead or board item.

Fluent Forms is another solid low cost option. It supports GTM, has native webhooks, and is easier to manage if you want a nicer UI.

If you want zero plugin cost, you can embed a monday WorkForm on your WordPress page and trigger GTM events at the page level, though it’s slightly more technical.

Most small teams I work with land on Contact Form 7 plus Make because it’s simple, reliable, and inexpensive.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Monday Wizard
